content.text:
## check.py
(minified; line numbers differ from disk)

import random
import unittest
import chess
import chess.pgn
import io
import json
import re
def run_solution(fen):
    for pattern, repl in json.load(open("/app/re.json")):
        fen = re.sub(pattern, repl, fen)
    fens = [" ".join(x.split(" ")[:-2]) for x in fen.split("\n")]
    return fens
class ChessGameTester:
    """Helper class to test move generation for any chess game."""
    def __init__(self):
        self.test_case = unittest.TestCase()
    def verify_legal_moves(self, fen):
        our_moves = run_solution(fen)
        board = chess.Board(fen)
        python_chess_moves = set()
        for move in board.legal_moves:
            if move.promotion and move.promotion != chess.QUEEN:
                continue
            board_copy = board.copy()
            board_copy.push(move)
            python_chess_moves.add(" ".join(board_copy.fen().split(" ")[:-2]))
        print(f"Position: {fen}, Our moves: {len(our_moves)}, Python-chess moves: {len(python_chess_moves)}")
        for x in our_moves:
            self.test_case.assertTrue(x in python_chess_moves or x.rpartition(" ")[0]+" -" in python_chess_moves, f"Position: {fen}, Our move: {x} not found in Python-chess moves:" + str(python_chess_moves))
        self.test_case.assertEqual(len(our_moves), len(python_chess_moves), f"Position: {fen}, Our moves: {len(our_moves)}, Python-chess moves: {len(python_chess_moves)}")
    def test_game(self, pgn_str, game_name="Unnamed Game"):
        """
        Test move generation for all positions in a chess game.
        
        Args:
            pgn_str: String containing the game in PGN format
            game_name: Name of the game for reporting purposes
        """
        print(f"\nTesting positions from: {game_name}")
        print("PGN string length:", len(pgn_str))
        pgn_str = pgn_str.strip().replace('\r\n', '\n')
        if pgn_str.startswith('\ufeff'):
            pgn_str = pgn_str[1:]
        game = chess.pgn.read_game(io.StringIO(pgn_str))
        if game is None:
            print(f"ERROR: Failed to parse PGN for game: {game_name}")
            print("Raw PGN string:")
            print(repr(pgn_str))
            raise ValueError(f"Failed to parse PGN for game: {game_name}")
        board = game.board()
        move_count = 0
        positions_tested = 0
        print("Testing initial position")
        self.verify_legal_moves(board.fen())
        positions_tested += 1
        mainline_moves = list(game.mainline_moves())
        print(f"Found {len(mainline_moves)} moves in the game")
        for move in mainline_moves:
            move_count += 1
            if move_count%2 == 1:
                fen = board.fen()
                print(f"Testing position after move {move_count}: {move.uci()}")
                self.verify_legal_moves(fen)
                positions_tested += 1
            board.push(move)
        print(f"Successfully tested {positions_tested} positions from {game_name} ({move_count} moves)")
class ChessGameTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.tester = ChessGameTester()
    def test_morphy_opera_game(self):
        pgn = """[Event "Opera Game"]
[Site "Paris FRA"]
[Date "1858.??.??"]
[Round "?"]
[White "Morphy, Paul"]
[Black "Duke of Brunswick & Count Isouard"]
[Result "1-0"]

1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 d6 3. d4 Bg4 4. dxe5 Bxf3 5. Qxf3 dxe5 6. Bc4 Nf6 7. Qb3 Qe7 8. Nc3 c6 9. Bg5 b5 10. Nxb5 cxb5 11. Bxb5+ Nbd7 12. O-O-O Rd8 13. Rxd7 Rxd7 14. Rd1 Qe6 15. Bxd7+ Nxd7 16. Qb8+ Nxb8 17. Rd8# 1-0"""
        self.tester.test_game(pgn, "Morphy's Opera Game")
def run_tests():
    suite = unittest.TestLoader().loadTestsFromTestCase(ChessGameTests)
    runner = unittest.TextTestRunner(failfast=True, verbosity=2)
    result = runner.run(suite)
    if not result.wasSuccessful():
        failure = result.failures[0] if result.failures else result.errors[0]
        test_case, traceback = failure
        print("\n\nFirst failing test:", test_case)
        print("\nError details:")
        print(traceback)
        exit(1)
if __name__ == '__main__':
    run_tests()

import random
import unittest
import chess
import chess.pgn
import io
import json
import re
def run_solution(fen):
    for pattern, repl in json.load(open("/app/re.json")):
        fen = re.sub(pattern, repl, fen)
    fens = [" ".join(x.split(" ")[:-2]) for x in fen.split("\n")]
    return fens
class ChessGameTester:
    """Helper class to test move generation for any chess game."""
    def __init__(self):
        self.test_case = unittest.TestCase()
    def verify_legal_moves(self, fen):
        our_moves = run_solution(fen)
        board = chess.Board(fen)
        python_chess_moves = set()
        for move in board.legal_moves:
            if move.promotion and move.promotion != chess.QUEEN:
                continue
            board_copy = board.copy()
            board_copy.push(move)
            python_chess_moves.add(" ".join(board_copy.fen().split(" ")[:-2]))
        print(f"Position: {fen}, Our moves: {len(our_moves)}, Python-chess moves: {len(python_chess_moves)}")
        for x in our_moves:
            self.test_case.assertTrue(x in python_chess_moves or x.rpartition(" ")[0]+" -" in python_chess_moves, f"Position: {fen}, Our move: {x} not found in Python-chess moves:" + str(python_chess_moves))
        self.test_case.assertEqual(len(our_moves), len(python_chess_moves), f"Position: {fen}, Our moves: {len(our_moves)}, Python-chess moves: {len(python_chess_moves)}")
    def test_game(self, pgn_str, game_name="Unnamed Game"):
        """
        Test move generation for all positions in a chess game.
        
        Args:
            pgn_str: String containing the game in PGN format
            game_name: Name of the game for reporting purposes
        """
        print(f"\nTesting positions from: {game_name}")
        print("PGN string length:", len(pgn_str))
        pgn_str = pgn_str.strip().replace('\r\n', '\n')
        if pgn_str.startswith('\ufeff'):
            pgn_str = pgn_str[1:]
        game = chess.pgn.read_game(io.StringIO(pgn_str))
        if game is None:
            print(f"ERROR: Failed to parse PGN for game: {game_name}")
            print("Raw PGN string:")
            print(repr(pgn_str))
            raise ValueError(f"Failed to parse PGN for game: {game_name}")
        board = game.board()
        move_count = 0
        positions_tested = 0
        print("Testing initial position")
        self.verify_legal_moves(board.fen())
        positions_tested += 1
        mainline_moves = list(game.mainline_moves())
        print(f"Found {len(mainline_moves)} moves in the game")
        for move in mainline_moves:
            move_count += 1
            if move_count%2 == 1:
                fen = board.fen()
                print(f"Testing position after move {move_count}: {move.uci()}")
                self.verify_legal_moves(fen)
                positions_tested += 1
            board.push(move)
        print(f"Successfully tested {positions_tested} positions from {game_name} ({move_count} moves)")
class ChessGameTests(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.tester = ChessGameTester()
    def test_morphy_opera_game(self):
        pgn = """[Event "Opera Game"]
[Site "Paris FRA"]
[Date "1858.??.??"]
[Round "?"]
[White "Morphy, Paul"]
[Black "Duke of Brunswick & Count Isouard"]
[Result "1-0"]

1. e4 e5 2. Nf3 d6 3. d4 Bg4 4. dxe5 Bxf3 5. Qxf3 dxe5 6. Bc4 Nf6 7. Qb3 Qe7 8. Nc3 c6 9. Bg5 b5 10. Nxb5 cxb5 11. Bxb5+ Nbd7 12. O-O-O Rd8 13. Rxd7 Rxd7 14. Rd1 Qe6 15. Bxd7+ Nxd7 16. Qb8+ Nxb8 17. Rd8# 1-0"""
        self.tester.test_game(pgn, "Morphy's Opera Game")
def run_tests():
    suite = unittest.TestLoader().loadTestsFromTestCase(ChessGameTests)
    runner = unittest.TextTestRunner(failfast=True, verbosity=2)
    result = runner.run(suite)
    if not result.wasSuccessful():
        failure = result.failures[0] if result.failures else result.errors[0]
        test_case, traceback = failure
        print("\n\nFirst failing test:", test_case)
        print("\nError details:")
        print(traceback)
        exit(1)
if __name__ == '__main__':
    run_tests()

content.text: `check.py` passes. Now broad adversarial stress test — random games + en-passant/promotion/castling-heavy positions:

input.command:
cd /app && python3 -c "
import json,re,random,chess
R=json.load(open('re.json'))
def run(fen):
    s=fen
    for p,r in R: s=re.sub(p,r,s)
    return [x for x in s.split('\n') if x]
def refset(fen):
    b=chess.Board(fen); out=set()
    for m in b.legal_moves:
        if m.promotion and m.promotion!=chess.QUEEN: continue
        c=b.copy(); c.push(m); out.add(' '.join(c.fen().split(' ')[:4]))
    return out
def norm(x):
    p=x.split(' '); return ' '.join(p[:4])
random.seed(1)
bad=0; tested=0
for g in range(400):
    b=chess.Board()
    depth=random.randint(0,40)
    for _ in range(depth):
        ms=list(b.legal_moves)
        if not ms: break
        b.push(random.choice(ms))
    if b.turn!=chess.WHITE: continue
    if b.is_game_over(): continue
    fen=b.fen()
     our=set()
    ok=True
    for x in run(fen):
        n=norm(x); our.add(n)
    ref=refset(fen)
    # apply the check.py forgiveness for ep
    tested+=1
    ours=set(run(fen))
    ourn=set(norm(x) for x in ours)
    # emulate check: each our move must be in ref OR (drop last token +' -') in ref
    fail=False
    for x in ourn:
        if x in ref: continue
        if x.rpartition(' ')[0]+' -' in ref: continue
        fail=True; print('EXTRA/BAD',fen,'::',x); break
    if len(ourn)!=len(ref):
        fail=True; print('COUNT',fen,len(ourn),len(ref))
    if fail: bad+=1
    if bad>=6: break
print('tested',tested,'bad',bad)
"

cd /app && python3 -c "
import json,re,random,chess
R=json.load(open('re.json'))
def run(fen):
    s=fen
    for p,r in R: s=re.sub(p,r,s)
    return [x for x in s.split('\n') if x]
def refset(fen):
    b=chess.Board(fen); out=set()
    for m in b.legal_moves:
        if m.promotion and m.promotion!=chess.QUEEN: continue
        c=b.copy(); c.push(m); out.add(' '.join(c.fen().split(' ')[:4]))
    return out
def norm(x): return ' '.join(x.split(' ')[:4])
random.seed(1)
bad=0; tested=0
for g in range(500):
    b=chess.Board(); depth=random.randint(0,50)
    for _ in range(depth):
        ms=list(b.legal_moves)
        if not ms: break
        b.push(random.choice(ms))
    if b.turn!=chess.WHITE or b.is_game_over(): continue
    fen=b.fen(); tested+=1
    ourn=set(norm(x) for x in run(fen)); ref=refset(fen)
    fail=False
    for x in ourn:
        if x in ref: continue
        if x.rpartition(' ')[0]+' -' in ref: continue
        fail=True; print('BAD ',fen,'::',x); break
    if len(ourn)!=len(ref):
        fail=True; print('COUNT',fen,len(ourn),len(ref))
    if fail:
        bad+=1
        if bad>=8: break
print('tested',tested,'bad',bad)
"

input.command:
cd /app && python3 -u -c "
import json,re,random,chess
R=json.load(open('re.json'))
def run(fen):
    s=fen
    for p,r in R: s=re.sub(p,r,s)
    return [x for x in s.split('\n') if x]
def refset(fen):
    b=chess.Board(fen); out=set()
    for m in b.legal_moves:
        if m.promotion and m.promotion!=chess.QUEEN: continue
        c=b.copy(); c.push(m); out.add(' '.join(c.fen().split(' ')[:4]))
    return out
def norm(x): return ' '.join(x.split(' ')[:4])
random.seed(1)
bad=0; tested=0
for g in range(500):
    b=chess.Board(); depth=random.randint(0,50)
    for _ in range(depth):
        ms=list(b.legal_moves)
        if not ms: break
        b.push(random.choice(ms))
    if b.turn!=chess.WHITE or b.is_game_over(): continue
    fen=b.fen(); tested+=1
    ourn=set(norm(x) for x in run(fen)); ref=refset(fen)
    fail=False
    for x in ourn:
        if x in ref or x.rpartition(' ')[0]+' -' in ref: continue
        fail=True; print('BAD ',fen,'::',x); break
    if len(ourn)!=len(ref):
        fail=True; print('COUNT',fen,len(ourn),len(ref))
    if fail:
        bad+=1
        if bad>=8: break
print('tested',tested,'bad',bad)
" 2>&1 | tail -20

cd /app && python3 -u -c "
import json,re,random,chess,time
R=json.load(open('re.json'))
def run(fen):
    s=fen
    for p,r in R: s=re.sub(p,r,s)
    return [x for x in s.split('\n') if x]
def refset(fen):
    b=chess.Board(fen); out=set()
    for m in b.legal_moves:
        if m.promotion and m.promotion!=chess.QUEEN: continue
        c=b.copy(); c.push(m); out.add(' '.join(c.fen().split(' ')[:4]))
    return out
def norm(x): return ' '.join(x.split(' ')[:4])
random.seed(1)
bad=0; tested=0; t0=time.time()
for g in range(150):
    b=chess.Board(); depth=random.randint(0,50)
    for _ in range(depth):
        ms=list(b.legal_moves)
        if not ms: break
        b.push(random.choice(ms))
    if b.turn!=chess.WHITE or b.is_game_over(): continue
    fen=b.fen(); tested+=1
    ourn=set(norm(x) for x in run(fen)); ref=refset(fen)
    fail=False
    for x in ourn:
        if x in ref or x.rpartition(' ')[0]+' -' in ref: continue
        fail=True; print('BAD ',fen,'::',x,flush=True); break
    if len(ourn)!=len(ref):
        fail=True; print('COUNT',fen,len(ourn),len(ref),flush=True)
    if fail:
        bad+=1
        if bad>=8: break
print('tested',tested,'bad',bad,'time',round(time.time()-t0,1),flush=True)
"

cd /app && python3 -u -c "
import json,re,chess
R=json.load(open('re.json'))
def run(fen):
    s=fen
    for p,r in R: s=re.sub(p,r,s)
    return [x for x in s.split('\n') if x]
def refset(fen):
    b=chess.Board(fen); out=set()
    for m in b.legal_moves:
        if m.promotion and m.promotion!=chess.QUEEN: continue
        c=b.copy(); c.push(m); out.add(' '.join(c.fen().split(' ')[:4]))
    return out
def norm(x): return ' '.join(x.split(' ')[:4])
tests=[
 'rnbqkbnr/ppp1p1pp/8/3pPp2/8/8/PPPP1PPP/RNBQKBNR w KQkq f6 0 3', # ep two options e5xf6, also d5 blocks
 '4k3/8/8/2pP4/8/8/8/4K3 w - c6 0 1', # ep with pin-ish
 '8/8/8/8/k1pP3R/8/8/4K3 w - c3 0 1', # ep exposing king? white Kd... rook
 'r3k2r/8/8/8/8/8/8/R3K2R w KQkq - 0 1', # both castle
 '4k3/8/8/8/8/8/8/R3K2R w KQ - 0 1',
 '4k3/8/8/8/8/8/8/R3K2r w Q - 0 1', # h1 rook is black-> no K right; queenside only
 'r3k2r/8/8/8/8/8/8/4K2R w Kkq - 0 1', # white a1 empty no Q
 '8/P6k/8/8/8/8/8/4K3 w - - 0 1', # promotion push
 '1n6/P6k/8/8/8/8/8/4K3 w - - 0 1', # promo capture b8 (rook? knight) - Q promo capture
 'r3k3/P7/8/8/8/8/8/4K3 w q - 0 1', # promo capture a8 rook removes q right
 '4k3/8/8/8/8/8/6p1/R3K2R w KQ - 0 1', # black pawn near
 '3rk3/8/8/8/8/8/8/R3K2R w KQ - 0 1', # d8 rook attacks d1? castle queenside thru d1
 '4k3/8/8/8/8/8/8/R3K2R w KQ - 0 1',
 '5k2/8/8/8/8/8/8/R3K2R w KQ - 0 1',
 '4rk2/8/8/8/8/8/8/R3K2R w KQ - 0 1', # e-file rook: king in check? e8 rook attacks e1 -> no castle either side
 '6k1/8/8/3pP3/8/8/8/4K3 w - d6 0 1',
]
bad=0
for fen in tests:
    ourn=set(norm(x) for x in run(fen)); ref=refset(fen)
    extra=[x for x in ourn if not(x in ref or x.rpartition(' ')[0]+' -' in ref)]
    miss=len(ourn)-len(ref)
    status='OK' if (not extra and len(ourn)==len(ref)) else 'FAIL'
    if status=='FAIL':
        bad+=1
        print(status,fen,'our',len(ourn),'ref',len(ref),'extra',extra[:3])
        # show missing
        refn=ref
        missing=[x for x in refn if x not in ourn and x.rpartition(' ')[0] not in [o.rpartition(' ')[0] for o in ourn]]
        print('   missing sample',missing[:3])
print('bad',bad,'of',len(tests))
"

All edge cases pass. Larger randomized batch (different seeds, check-heavy):

input.command:
cd /app && python3 -u -c "
import json,re,random,chess,time
R=json.load(open('re.json'))
def run(fen):
    s=fen
    for p,r in R: s=re.sub(p,r,s)
    return [x for x in s.split('\n') if x]
def refset(fen):
    b=chess.Board(fen); out=set()
    for m in b.legal_moves:
        if m.promotion and m.promotion!=chess.QUEEN: continue
        c=b.copy(); c.push(m); out.add(' '.join(c.fen().split(' ')[:4]))
    return out
def norm(x): return ' '.join(x.split(' ')[:4])
bad=0; tested=0; chk=0; t0=time.time()
for seed in range(2,9):
    random.seed(seed)
    for g in range(60):
        b=chess.Board(); depth=random.randint(4,60)
        for _ in range(depth):
            ms=list(b.legal_moves)
            if not ms: break
            b.push(random.choice(ms))
        if b.turn!=chess.WHITE or b.is_game_over(): continue
        fen=b.fen(); tested+=1
        if b.is_check(): chk+=1
        ourn=set(norm(x) for x in run(fen)); ref=refset(fen)
        for x in ourn:
            if x in ref or x.rpartition(' ')[0]+' -' in ref: continue
            print('BAD',fen,'::',x,flush=True); bad+=1; break
        else:
            if len(ourn)!=len(ref):
                print('COUNT',fen,len(ourn),len(ref),flush=True); bad+=1
        if bad>=10: break
    if bad>=10: break
print('tested',tested,'inCheck',chk,'bad',bad,'time',round(time.time()-t0,1),flush=True)
"

input.command:
cd /app && timeout 240 python3 -u -c "
import json,re,random,chess,time,sys
R=json.load(open('re.json'))
def run(fen):
    s=fen
    for p,r in R: s=re.sub(p,r,s)
    return [x for x in s.split('\n') if x]
def refset(fen):
    b=chess.Board(fen); out=set()
    for m in b.legal_moves:
        if m.promotion and m.promotion!=chess.QUEEN: continue
        c=b.copy(); c.push(m); out.add(' '.join(c.fen().split(' ')[:4]))
    return out
def norm(x): return ' '.join(x.split(' ')[:4])
bad=0; tested=0; chk=0
for seed in range(2,7):
    random.seed(seed)
    for g in range(50):
        b=chess.Board(); depth=random.randint(4,60)
        for _ in range(depth):
            ms=list(b.legal_moves)
            if not ms: break
            b.push(random.choice(ms))
        if b.turn!=chess.WHITE or b.is_game_over(): continue
        fen=b.fen(); tested+=1
        if b.is_check(): chk+=1
        ourn=set(norm(x) for x in run(fen)); ref=refset(fen)
        ok=True
        for x in ourn:
            if x in ref or x.rpartition(' ')[0]+' -' in ref: continue
            sys.stdout.write('BAD %s :: %s\n'%(fen,x)); ok=False; break
        if ok and len(ourn)!=len(ref):
            sys.stdout.write('COUNT %s %d %d\n'%(fen,len(ourn),len(ref))); ok=False
        if not ok: bad+=1
sys.stdout.write('RESULT tested %d inCheck %d bad %d\n'%(tested,chk,bad))
" 2>&1
